description_long: This object is a taxidermy duck mounted on a rectangular wooden base, likely intended for display. The duck has a dark, almost black head and upper body, with a lighter, creamy white underside and a distinct white patch on its side. Its beak is slightly open and pointed upward, and the eyes are glassy, giving a lifelike appearance. The wings are folded against the body, and the feet are visible, attached to the wooden base. The wooden base is simple, with visible grain and a natural finish, supporting the duck in an upright, alert posture. The overall appearance suggests a preserved specimen, commonly found in educational or decorative urban settings.
description: A lifelike taxidermy duck with a dark head and back, creamy underside, and mounted on a rectangular wooden base.
description_view_0: The duck is seen from a low front-left angle, highlighting its upward-pointed beak, glassy eye, and the contrast between its dark upper body and pale underside.
description_view_1: A side view showing the full profile of the duck, the folded wings, and the attachment of its feet to the wooden base.
description_view_2: A top-down view emphasizing the dark coloration of the head and back, as well as the rectangular shape of the wooden base.
description_view_3: A rear-side angle showing the back of the duck, the tail, and the mounting of the feet to the base.
